item.title: These are the kind of National Parks videos I want
item.dc:creator: countervail247365

item.title: Happily unproductive
item.description: My wife was gone for 4 days, and I made myself a good-sized "honey do" list, but my kids kept me busy wanting to hang out with me. Those of you with teenagers will know that this isn't always the case. Got some really good time together: talking about random things (they know I'll answer any question they ask, if i know the answer), cooking, baking, playing board games and Nintendo Switch, and watching movies. It's not like we never do that stuff together, but it was a lot more than usual. I'm a very happy dad right now :) EDIT- yay viral post. Thanks, everyone, for the great comments. It's not all fun and games, but you gotta take the little things as the years pass too quickly. EDIT EDIT - Couple people mentioned 'cleanliness', so I guess I should elaborate Projects list - taking down Christmas tree, putting together a new cabinet, clearing out space in the garage, organizing a couple closets, and building a new wooden dog crate. Only 1 of these got done Standard adulting (imo) - dishes, laundry, trash taken out, sweeping, and mopping. Only the mopping didn't get done because 4 dogs and snow makes that silly in my eyes. The sink was empty and only 1 of the 10 loads of laundry was left not put away.

item.title: Splendor in the Dump
item.dc:creator: derek237

item.title: What a way to start the week...
item.description: My mom lost the battle with cancer this morning, and I'm struggling. She was my favorite person in the world. I wasn't ready to lose her at 26 years old. I'm watching Charmed right now. It was the show we watched together when I was growing up. I feel so empty. I could use some help cheering up a bit, maybe send some memes. Miss you, mom. EDIT: Never expected to get Most Viral. Thank you to everyone for the well wishes and even the bits of dark humour. I appreciate you all so much, what a great community.
item.dc:creator: NLMA9127

item.title: The 386th vol. of "I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps - DOGS WITH SOUND Edition #3
item.description:
DATA WARNING! ALL VIDEO DUMP!
STAY TUNED! I'll be releasing more MeeMays back into the wild Monday-Friday around 11:30 am, GMT-5, in my ""I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so that I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps" Or use Imgur's "Follow" Feature, which will alert you whenever one of my dumps goes Viral.
This is my newly commissioned Signature Image, by Dark Arts Studios. He's no longer on The Imgurs, I met him on ElbowDeepInAHorse's Discord Server. Here's where you can see his work: https://twitter.com/Anubis23342060 https://www.furaffinity.net/user/thed4rk1ord/ https://tailsofthegods.com/ https://dark-arts.itch.io/ FUN TIP / LIFE FACT / PRO HACK I upvote 90% - 99% of positive or neutral comments on my posts, as well as ≈50% of negative ones Shoutout to my fellow Dumpers: @DeafKitten @CodyBurkett @OhValleyOfPlenty @AbbiiStabbii @DigiT00l @MemeMuseumCurator @CeleryMan7 @DumpsterKoala @JebidiahBeetus @Kaeldra @KirbyVictorious @HermioneDanger00 @DumpReview @WhiskeyJelly @JasonPositivityPilot @danat101 @PotatoCheesMonk @VanessaNelson @NotMuchBiggerThanAWomprat @MindNumbingBraIncAndy @TheMellowMoron @TheWoodsAreVile @ThatOtherGirlYouKnow @IStealMemesAndStuff @DrWalters @DaymanAhAAAAah Lemme know If you are a Regular Dumper and would like to be added to the list! Archive Of Previous Dumps: Vols 1-300: https://imgur.com/a/vAfF9Wz Vols 301+ : https://imgur.com/a/oDakPaq Updated Whenever I Can Get Around To It #MemeDump #Puppers #Doggos #PuppersAndDoggos #AdolfHitlerSchoolForTheArts #TheWhoring20s #SaveTheBEEEEEES #BeeLM #SaveTheBees #JettfreyEpsfuelCandnt #CommeltSteelBeamicide #WhatIfNobodyShotJFK #AndHisHeadJustDidThat #EatingAppsTakingNaps #EatNamesAndTakeAss #MasturbatingIsNecrophilia #IfYoureDeadInside #DumpyMcGeeAndGuyNamedSteve #2Dumps1Site #AllTiddiesAreGoodTiddies #FreeTheNipple

item.title: Happy Martin Luther King Day to everyone who knows what he was really about.
item.description: Regan's involvement of MLK day should be the first red flag. Read more here: https://www.nytimes.com/1983/10/22/us/reagan-s-doubts-on-dr-king-disclosed.html https://www.bostonreview.net/articles/christopher-petrella-reagan-created-mlk-day-because-he-hated-mlk/ https://daily.jstor.org/ronald-reagan-martin-luther-king/

item.title: Trapped, lonely, and depressed
item.description: This will die, I hope, in usersub. I just need to vent my misery to the void. 18 months ago, I decided I wanted to divorce my wife of 13 years. This was after almost 7 years of dead bedroom, two rounds of marriage counseling, begging and pleading for her to meet me even a quarter of the way. I couldn't go through with it. I bitched out because I didn't want to hurt her. She has her head in the sand and thinks everything is fine. Outside of the marriage, I never really had real friends. I used to have some work friends, but I took a promotion a while ago and with covid and everyone working from home most of the time, I never see them and when I do it's awkward because of the promotion. I have always been very introverted. To top it off, I grew up in a very repressed household. We didn't express our emotions. As an adult, I have trouble expressing my emotions. I asked for therapy from my health care provider and they gave me a referral for a place 2 hours away that has a 2.0 star rating on Google. What the fuck good is that? Everything seems so hopeless. I'm trapped in a marriage I don't want, I'm not free to pursue other interests, I can't figure out how to make friends, I'm trapped in my job by golden handcuffs. Increasingly, the only way out seems like ending it. I think about it daily. The loneliness is almost unbearable. My kids are the only thing keeping me here. I don't want to hurt them. But they know I'm unhappy. I try to put on a good face with them, but it's getting harder and harder to do. I'm tired. I'm sick of being abused. I'm sick of being used. I'm sick of feeling hopeless. I'm sick of not being able to do the things I want to do or experience the things I want to experience because I'm stuck with someone. I'm just so over it. Yes, this is fixable. Yes, it's on me to fix it. I just don't fucking know how. Good night, Imgur.
item.dc:creator: everythingisawful123

item.title: He also killed Mr Peanut with an Apache helicopter?!

item.title: Just a little meme dump, as a treat
item.description:
I do love a good jar. Yesterday I seriously considered buying something I don’t need just because it came in a dope jar.
America is cold af right now
It just goes to the chair (you know which chair)
Fuck dem Fascists
What women want
Hurt people hurt people: testosterone edition
I don’t know who these people are that are fine with peeing a thousand times a day, but I ain’t got time for that. If I’m thirsty, I drink. Seems like overkill to force it.
Fun fact: solitary bees (and wasps) are MUCH more efficient pollinators than honey bees, they just aren’t easy to capitalize on commercially so they don’t get nearly the attention and support they should.
Hope it’s got some of those cheddar biscuit smells still
Nature is endlessly beautiful and I am here on earth to experience it
I know most of y’all checking in from the toilet. Don’t push too hard and wash your goddamn hands afterwards!
item.dc:creator: KlvrDissident

item.title: I renovated my attic and improved the insulation
item.description:
This is the newly renovated attic of our house in France. I had a few goals with this, not all of which were totally sensible: 1. First up, improve the insulation. During heatwaves, the upstairs can get pretty unliveable. We are trying to make do without aircon - shuttered windows and keeping the house cool and dark work much better than you'd think, but it can only do so much in a heatwave. 2. Make it easy to move around up there. Just for peace of mind, in case of leaks, broken tiles, future solar panel installation, etc 3. Reduce the amount of dust, leaves etc which get blown in
This is the before shot. Kind of reminds me of those pictures taken from the surface of a comet. Cobwebs, dust, leaves everywhere, and incredibly uneven blown-in insulation.
Another angle. It was certainly well ventilated!
Look at all that dust floating around! This was definitely a PPE zone. (Mask, goggles, gloves, full overalls)
Closeup showing how the roof angles down past the insulation, forming a soffit at the end.
A majot issue with the renovation was the entrance - only 50x52cm, so getting large items up was a challenge. Also look how there is nothing blocking the insulation so it kind of just peters out to about 1cm depth near the entrance.
The fight begins! First step was to go to war with the cobwebs. For this I just used a rag, PPE and some good podcasts. My mask was disgusting after this but it made the space workable. I also did a first pass at levelling out the existing insulation.
More of the insulation after being levelled. The rake wasn't working too well for me so I was mostly lying down on planks laid across the beams and doing it by hand. Fun times.
After levelling everything and finding I had about 10cm average, I tackled the entrance. The 'double box' shape is due to the back of the entrance not being next to a supporting beam. The two long sections hang off the beams, with notches cut out so that the shorter sections can hang off them. I caulked to make the seal as tight as possible. The box is 75mm higher than the insulation/beams as that was the thickness of the top-up insulation I had chosen.
Starting on a 'crawl way' along the length of the attic. 75x36mm planks laid edge-on across the beams, each lying on three beams total. The width between was 130cm, or exactly two of the insulating batts. (see at end). This also shows the new lights I put in! II saw a great idea which was not to attempt to hook lights up to the mains, and instead just treat it like a large lampshade, and have an extra long cable and a plug which can plug into the nearest socket downstairs. This way it is only connected when you are in the attic and is not a fire risk. I hooked up a bunch of light sockets and ran the cable behind some thin white hardboard cut to fit each section.
After filling in between the crawl way 'walls' I laid 18mm OSB over the top, using an online calculator to ensure this thickness was sufficient. I wanted to give the junction box a bit of air so I lifted it up off the floor of the attic with a plank braced between two beams, and placed loose insulation underneath.
The OSB was the first major issue as its width of 67cm would technically fit through the entrance, but practically could not due to banging into tiles etc above. So each piece needed to be cut in half. This significantly reduced its strength (particularly as they were interlocking) so I screwed down (I think 24x27mm) lengths of wood as a 'spine' which stiffened everything up a lot.
The next step was to add a breathable membrane under the tiles. Usually this is done during house construction and is placed over the stringers, which is a whole lot easier than stapling it from underneath - this is a backbreaking job involving being stretched out long planks laid between beams with zero headroom with a staple gun in improbably yoga positions. The result is imperfect, but will prevent a large amound of leaves and dust from making it in while still allowing the attic space to breath. At the end you can see the 'baffle' I installed, which is just a piece of literally anything which directs airflow from the soffit up and away from the insulation before allowing it into the attic space. This apparently helps reduce cooling of the insulation in winter. I used expanded foam insulation as it was the cheapest solid item I could find in the store.
The last step was then to install the rest of the batt insulation. This involved meticulously and tediously cutting around all the beams etc that were in the way. Batt insulation like this is usually used for walls - rolls or blown-in insulation is normally preferred for attic floors. I used batts because blown-in would ruin the nice clean result I was going for, and rolls were too big to fit in the entrance. This works, it just takes time and patience to reduce gaps to an absolute minimum.
The easy bit of the breathable membrane done - time for more yoga!
Membranes and baffles all done, most of the batts up in the space.
Close-up of the cut-away work around the exhaust system.
Final result.
Bit of a vid.
Shot showing how the 'sea level' of the insulation is now at the height of the entrance I constructed all-those-weekends ago. Much thicker, hopefully much toastier in winter and cooler in summer! Thanks for taking a look!
item.dc:creator: macamat

item.title: Yesterday at work
item.description: We saw the clouds movimg in,but we didn't expect it would be a storm like this. Right after this I grabbed some rope to tie it tho the pushback tractor. We still held on to it for about half an hour. The wind was exceptionally strong. Weather cleared and the plane did land safelly. Santo Ângelo, Brazil.
item.dc:creator: 5pd5tr

item.title: Reminder:
item.description: The actions of the Israeli government and the IDF do not represent all Jews any more than Hamas' actions represent all Palestinians. There is not some secret Jewish cabal or hivemenind controlling Western governments or media. If anything, Western governments empower Israel as a means to exert geopolitical influence in the MiddleEast, not the other way around. Some of the talking points I'm seeing are getting dangerously close to gross conspiratorial bullshit they spread in altright/ neonazi circles.
item.dc:creator: Decitron
